## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On August 23, 1939, an incredibly massive, absolutely shocking, and historically terrifying diplomatic bombshell completely shattered the global geopolitical architecture. Nazi Germany and the Soviet Union officially and aggressively signed the Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact in Moscow. This massive, incredibly cynical non-aggression treaty between bitter ideological enemies fiercely included a highly secret protocol to aggressively partition Poland and Eastern Europe between them. This monumental, massive betrayal absolutely paved the direct, unstoppable path for Hitler's invasion of Poland just days later, igniting World War II.

On August 23, 1990, the incredibly massive, heavily divided nations of West Germany and East Germany officially and aggressively announced a massive, historic agreement regarding their absolute, impending reunification. Following the incredible, massive collapse of the Berlin Wall the previous year, the two states formally set October 3 as the absolute date for full political and economic integration. This massive, monumental diplomatic triumph fundamentally and aggressively erased the absolute, primary geographic fault line of the Cold War in Europe.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On August 23, 1947, Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, the incredibly massive, fiercely unyielding, and absolute "Iron Man of India," was officially and aggressively appointed as the Deputy Prime Minister of the newly independent Indian dominion. Alongside his massive role as the Minister of Home Affairs, Patel utilized his absolute, uncompromising political brilliance and aggressive diplomatic coercion to heavily force the integration of over 500 massive, independent princely states into the Indian Union, fundamentally securing the absolute, massive territorial integrity of the modern republic.

On August 23, 1872, Tanguturi Prakasam, the incredibly massive, highly revered, and fiercely brave independence activist famously known as "Andhra Kesari" (Lion of Andhra), was officially born. He achieved absolute, immortal historical fame by aggressively and fearlessly baring his chest to the heavily armed British colonial police during the massive, fierce protests against the Simon Commission in Madras in 1928. His incredibly massive, uncompromising political leadership heavily propelled him to become the absolute, very first Chief Minister of the newly created Andhra State in 1953.
